# Copilot CLI allowlist + enforcement (`home-ops`)

Use these as pre-approvable commands **only when** `KUBECONFIG` points to the scoped read-only config generated by `scripts/generate-copilot-kubeconfig.sh`.

## Enforce it (recommended)

Use `scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh` as a required command wrapper in your Copilot CLI / `ghc` setup, so commands are denied in-process instead of just documented.

Example shell aliases:

```sh
alias kubectl='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh kubectl'
alias flux='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh flux'
alias flux-local='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh flux-local'
alias helm='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh helm'
alias kustomize='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh kustomize'
alias git='scripts/copilot-command-guard.sh git'
```

The guard script denies mutating commands and egress tools (`curl`/`wget`/`nc`/`ssh`/`scp`) with exit code `126`.

## Allowlist (safe/read-only)

- Kubernetes discovery and inspection:
    - `kubectl get ...`
    - `kubectl describe ...`
    - `kubectl logs ...`
    - `kubectl top ...`
    - `kubectl explain ...`
    - `kubectl api-resources`
    - `kubectl api-versions`
    - `kubectl version`
    - `kubectl config view --minify`
- Flux read-only:
    - `flux get ...`
    - `flux tree ...`
    - `flux stats`
    - `flux check`
    - `flux diff ...`
    - `flux trace ...`
- Local GitOps rendering/analysis:
    - `flux-local test ...`
    - `flux-local diff ...`
    - `flux-local build ...`
    - `kustomize build ...`
    - `kubeconform ...`
    - `helm template ...`
    - `helm show ...`
    - read-only git commands: `git status`, `git diff`, `git show`, `git log`

`yq`, `jq`, `rg`, `grep`, and `find` are intentionally **not** allowed by the guard wrapper; run them only with explicit approval.

## Require approval / denylist

- Any mutating kubectl verb/subcommand:
    - `apply`, `delete`, `edit`, `patch`, `replace`, `create`, `scale`, `rollout`, `drain`, `cordon`, `uncordon`, `label`, `annotate`
    - `cp`, `exec`, `port-forward`, `proxy`, `debug`, `attach`
- Any mutating Flux action:
    - `flux reconcile`, `flux suspend`, `flux resume`, `flux create`, `flux delete`
- Any mutating Helm action:
    - `helm install`, `helm upgrade`, `helm uninstall`, `helm rollback`
- Network/egress or remote-access tools:
    - `curl`, `wget`, `nc`, `ssh`, `scp`
- Any command writing outside the repo working tree.
